practical_and_experimental_content: Demonstrate the use of various measuring instruments (rules, vernier calipers, micrometer screw gauge) to determine length.
Demonstrate the use of clocks and devices for measuring an interval of time.
Investigate factors that affect the period of a simple pendulum.
Demonstrate the relationship between force, mass and acceleration.
Investigate the turning effect of a force (Hooke's Law).
Demonstrate the use of simple machines to do work.
Demonstrate an appreciation of safety in the laboratory and apply general laboratory safety rules.

topics.topic: Chemistry: 10.2 The Particulate Nature of Matter
practical_and_experimental_content: Demonstrate the changes of states of matter (melting, freezing, boiling, condensation, sublimation).
Demonstrate the movement of particles (diffusion) in fluids.
Demonstrate how different quantities are measured (time, temperature, mass, and volume).
Identify and safely use measuring apparatus.
Demonstrate determination of the purity of a substance using melting and boiling points.
Demonstrate different methods of separating mixtures (decantation, filtration, crystallisation, distillation, magnetism, chromatography).
Interpret simple paper chromatograms.

topics.topic: Chemistry: 10.4 Atoms, Elements, Compounds and Molecules

subtopics: 11.3.3 Expansion of Solids, Liquids and Gases
11.3.5 Heat transfer by Conduction, Convection and Radiation
practical_and_experimental_content: Measure the temperature with thermometers.
Experiment with the thermal expansion of matter (liquid, solid, gases).
Demonstrate heat conduction in different substances.
Demonstrate the uses of bad and good conductors of heat.
Demonstrate convection in liquids and gases.
Experiment to determine the speed of sound in air.
Investigate the reflection and refraction of light.
Perform experiments to trace the path of light through a glass block and a prism.
Demonstrate the making and destroying of magnets.
Plot magnetic field lines.

subtopics: 11.5.1 Characteristic properties of acids and bases
11.6.4 Identification of ions and gases (Qualitative analysis)
practical_and_experimental_content: Investigate the acidity and alkalinity of substances.
Demonstrate the preparation of salts (soluble and insoluble) by precipitation, reactions of acids with bases, metals, and carbonates.
Perform qualitative analysis to identify unknown cations, anions, and gases.
Demonstrate acid-base titrations to determine concentration.
Determine the rate of chemical reactions experimentally.
Investigate the physical and chemical properties of transition elements experimentally.
Demonstrate the existence of static charges.
Explain how to detect electric charges using an electroscope.

subtopics: 12.9.1 Electric charge, current, and potential difference
12.9.4 Heating effect of an electric current
practical_and_experimental_content: Demonstrate how to determine resistance in a simple circuit.
Measure current and voltage.
Experiment with the magnetic field patterns of electric currents.

subtopics: 12.10.2 The simple A.C. and D.C. generators
practical_and_experimental_content: Demonstrate the phenomenon of electromagnetic induction.
Investigate properties of cathode rays by using a Cathode Ray Oscilloscope (CRO).
Demonstrate safety precautions when handling or storing radioactive substances.
Demonstrate an order of reactivity from a set of experimental results.
Investigate methods of preventing corrosion.
Demonstrate the laboratory methods for the preparation, collection, and testing of common gases.
Describe and conduct the chemical tests for unsaturated hydrocarbons (alkenes) using bromine solution.
Demonstrate the structures of isomers of alkenes using models.
